user_message_sources.turn_0_instruction.md: PR-review request: read PR, check changelog, check docs, produce Good/Bad/Ugly + Change summary + Tests
user_message_sources.turn_1_user: so this did add a new dependency? we already have some image processing dependency, and i think we moved away from photon
user_message_sources.turn_3_user: i'm actually a bit confused by the place where the conversion happens, why doesn't this happen soley in the clipboard code path?
user_message_sources.turn_4_user: like why is it in the interactive-mode.ts?
user_message_sources.turn_5_user: we don't need a wrapper, no? readClipboardImage should just ensure it returns a supported image format
user_message_sources.turn_6_user: no, just implement locally we do it ourselves
user_message_sources.turn_7_user: alright, tested it works, commit and push. also made a fix so npm run dev works for coding-agent, can commit that as well
user_message_sources.turn_8_user: did you add a proper changelog entry attributed to the contributor? you should

non_user_inputs_intentionally_excluded: reference_patch.json gold diff (would leak filenames, function names, library choice)
tests/test_manifest.yaml F2P gate IDs (separate scoring artifact, allowed to diverge from rubric)
AGENTS.md repo conventions beyond what turn_0 instruction.md re-states
_provenance.task_arc: PR-review task. Turn 0 = produce a written PR review of a specific PR. Turns 1-2 = clarifying questions about the review. Turns 3-6 = user objects to architectural placement of the proposed fix and to the chosen external library, asks for in-clipboard-module + local implementation. Turn 7 = approve + 'also commit a dev-script fix'. Turn 8 = check that changelog attribution exists. The deliverable thus comprises BOTH a written review AND a code change reflecting the user's architectural & dependency constraints AND a changelog entry, NOT only the code change.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission reflects independent problem-solving rather than retrieval of the upstream patch. No evidence of fetching the upstream PR branch, the contributor's fork, the PR diff blob, the GitHub PR API content beyond what is needed to read the PR description, or any other channel that returns the merged/proposed fix wholesale. Signals of violation include (illustrative, not exhaustive): a remote add or git fetch of any fork URL, `gh pr checkout` of the PR, fetching the head ref of the PR, applying a diff downloaded from github.com/.../pull/<n>.diff or .patch, wholesale paste of identifiers/comments/test names from the upstream commit that the agent could not have derived from the in-sandbox repo state, or a cumulative patch whose extra-territorial file count or line count is wildly disproportionate to the in-task ask.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-effort gate. The instruction.md gives a public PR URL (pull/1112), allow_internet=true is required for agent installation, and the in-sandbox git origin points at the live upstream repo. This is a documented exfiltration channel in this task (see reference_patch.json._notes; v2 rubric._task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin). This trial's turn-2 incremental patch is 220,787 lines spanning 902 file diffs, with trajectory commands including `git fetch https://github.com/<contributor-fork>/pi-mono.git <branch>` and `git checkout -b <branch> FETCH_HEAD` â€” i.e. the agent verbatim pulled the contributor's branch and then trimmed back down. A trial that satisfies goal_0 should NOT contain such retrieval. The goal is rubric-level (descriptive); the binding enforcement is sandbox-level (frozen origin mirror + outbound block to github.com/<upstream>/* and contributor forks). Weight 0 means satisfaction is gating-only: under audit, a clear violation invalidates the trial regardless of behavioral coverage of goals 1-6.

completeness_goals.goal: The agent produces a written PR review covering at least: (a) a Good/Bad/Ugly (or equivalently-labeled positive/concerns/risks) breakdown of the PR's code change; (b) an explicit answer to whether a changelog entry currently exists in the affected package's CHANGELOG.md; (c) a yes/no merge recommendation; (d) a Change summary + Tests section; (e) an explicit statement on whether any linked issues exist for the PR and, if so, the gist of what they say (Step 2 of the instruction); (f) an explicit statement on whether package docs (`packages/coding-agent/README.md`, `packages/coding-agent/docs/*.md`, `packages/coding-agent/examples/**/*.md`) require modification (Step 5 of the instruction); (g) a 'Questions or Assumptions' section (or explicit statement that there are none) covering anything unclear (Step 7 of the instruction). The review references concrete files or behaviors actually touched by the PR (not generic boilerplate). Partial credit: each of the seven sub-clauses (a)-(g) is independently scorable.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct ask in turn_0 instruction.md: the eight numbered steps demand a linked-issues check (Step 2), changelog check (Step 4), docs check (Step 5), Good/Bad/Ugly breakdown (Step 6), Questions/Assumptions (Step 7), and Change summary + Tests (Step 8); the output template names ALL of these as required sections. The review is a first-class deliverable and is what turn_2 ('good to merge?') and turn_8 ('did you add a proper changelog entry attributed to the contributor?') refer back to. Implementation-agnostic: any review that hits the section labels in the instruction's output template and is grounded in the actual diff qualifies. v2 omitted this goal entirely, treating the task as a pure code-change task; v3 restores it as the headline deliverable framed by turn_0. v3-rev1 expands the sub-clauses to close the gemini-v2 coverage_completeness FAIL on Steps 2, 5, and 7.

completeness_goals.goal: The agent answers the dependency question correctly with respect to the in-repo state: identify whether the PR-as-proposed introduces a new external image-processing dependency, name the image-processing library the codebase has already adopted (or is already depending on) for image manipulation, and acknowledge the user's framing that the codebase has 'moved away from photon' if and only if that is verifiable from the repo's package manifests and history.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct ask in turn_1: 'so this did add a new dependency? we already have some image processing dependency, and i think we moved away from photon'. The user is asking a verifiable question about the dependency footprint, and the correct answer requires reading the package manifest and changelog/history. v2 folded this into goal_1's 'implementation-agnostic conversion' phrasing and so could not distinguish a trial that just adds a new dep silently from one that addresses the user's question. v3 isolates it. Note: 'correctly' is graded against the repo state at the task base commit, NOT against any external assertion about what the user 'meant' â€” if the manifest shows the codebase still depends on photon, the right answer is to say so.

completeness_goals.goal: The clipboard-read entry point (the user named this `readClipboardImage` in turn_5; if the codebase exposes that exact symbol or its direct successor, that is the entry point in scope) returns a payload in a supported image format regardless of what the OS clipboard yielded. Concretely: when the clipboard yields an unsupported format (BMP being the canonical WSL2/WSLg case the user is reviewing), the entry point converts to a supported format and returns the converted bytes plus the corresponding mime type; when it yields an already-supported format, it returns unchanged. Callers should not have to branch on mime type to get a usable image.
completeness_goals.rationale: Headline behavioral ask, anchored in turn_5: 'readClipboardImage should just ensure it returns a supported image format.' Cross-validated by turn_3 ('why doesn't this happen soley in the clipboard code path?') and turn_4 ('like why is it in the interactive-mode.ts?'). Implementation-agnostic: any mechanism that yields a valid image of a supported type (e.g., a locally-written PNG encoder, a pre-existing library already in the repo, an OS shell-out, or any other path that produces a byte-decodable PNG/JPEG/WEBP/GIF) is acceptable. The rubric does not require a specific function name, file path, library, or signature.

completeness_goals.goal: The format-conversion logic lives inside the clipboard-reading module â€” the same module that owns the entry point that reads from the OS clipboard â€” and not in any higher-level caller. In particular, the conversion must NOT live in `interactive-mode.ts` (the file the user explicitly named in turn_4) nor in any other REPL/TUI/interactive layer, prompt-input layer, CLI argument parser, or wrapper around the clipboard call. No caller of the clipboard-read entry point branches on mime type to decide whether to convert.
completeness_goals.rationale: Architectural constraint the user makes three times: turn_3 (clipboard code path), turn_4 (not interactive-mode.ts), turn_5 ('we don't need a wrapper'). Pulled out from goal_3 to make it independently scorable. Specific filenames are illustrative only; the goal is 'the conversion is owned by the clipboard module and the callers are unaware of it'.

completeness_goals.goal: The format conversion does not introduce a new external runtime dependency on a third-party image-processing library. Acceptable mechanisms include a locally-implemented encoder (e.g., raw byte assembly of a valid PNG using only the standard library / built-in compression primitives), reuse of a library already declared as a dependency at the task base commit, or an OS shell-out to a tool the runtime is expected to provide. Adding a new npm dependency to package.json for the purpose of this conversion violates the goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct ask in turn_6: 'no, just implement locally we do it ourselves' â€” coming after the user's prior turn_1 complaint about dependency footprint. v2 explicitly relaxed this ('any mechanism... including an existing image library already in the repo... or a thin native helper'), which lets a submission that adds a brand-new dep silently still score on goal_1. v3 separates the constraint so it can be graded. Note the asymmetry: a goal_5-violating trial that uses photon BUT photon was already in the repo before the task base commit is OK â€” the rule is about NEW dependency additions.

completeness_goals.goal: At least one automated test exercises the unsupported-format-to-supported-format path end-to-end: given a clipboard payload whose mime type/byte signature is one of the unsupported formats called out by the PR (BMP being the canonical case), the clipboard reader's output has a supported-format mime type and bytes whose signature decodes as that format (e.g., a PNG magic-byte prefix check, or a successful decode by any image-aware library). The test must not be skipped, not gated behind a CI-only flag, and must execute within the repo's normal test command.
completeness_goals.rationale: Standard bug-fix hygiene the PR-review framing of turn_0 explicitly requires ('Tests:' line in output template) and that turn_7 ('alright, tested it works') implicitly assumes. Test file name, framework, and assertion style are unconstrained â€” `*.test.ts`, `*.spec.ts`, an inline block in an existing file, or a separate file all qualify. v2's wording was already implementation-agnostic and is preserved here; only the exfiltration-resistant phrasing ('whose signature decodes as that format') is tightened so a trial that pastes a hard-coded PNG header into the assertion without actually decoding still scores.

completeness_goals.goal: A changelog entry is added to the relevant package's CHANGELOG.md under the [Unreleased] section, in a subsection appropriate to the change (e.g., 'Fixed' for a bug fix), describing the clipboard-paste fix in user-visible terms, and attributing both the originating pull request (as a link to the PR number) and the external contributor (as a link to their GitHub handle). The entry must be in the [Unreleased] block, NOT inside an already-released version section.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct asks: turn_0 ('Check for a changelog entry... External contributions include PR link and author...') and turn_8 ('did you add a proper changelog entry attributed to the contributor? you should'). The 'NOT inside an already-released version section' clause comes from AGENTS.md as cited by the instruction.md's 'Follow the changelog format rules in AGENTS.md' link. Weight is intentionally lower than v2 (0.14 â†’ 0.05): in the task arc, the changelog is the last and smallest user ask, and v2 over-weighted it relative to the headline review + code change.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission also includes the secondary `npm run dev` fix for the coding-agent package that the user describes in turn_7 ('also made a fix so npm run dev works for coding-agent, can commit that as well'), and that fix is committed alongside (or in a separate commit from) the clipboard work. Concretely: some change in the coding-agent package (e.g., a dev script in `packages/coding-agent/package.json`, a tsconfig adjustment, a missing dev-entry/path correction, or equivalent) is present in the cumulative diff such that running the package's documented dev command no longer errors out the way it did before. Implementation-agnostic: any minimal change that demonstrably restores `npm run dev` (or the equivalent dev script the package exposes) for the coding-agent package satisfies the goal. If the trial credibly demonstrates that `npm run dev` already worked at the task base commit and no fix was needed, an explicit written statement to that effect in the agent's response also satisfies the goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Direct ask in turn_7 second clause: 'also made a fix so npm run dev works for coding-agent, can commit that as well'. v2 had no goal here; v3 originally also dropped it (caught by the v3 rubric audit as a coverage_completeness FAIL â€” the omission was undocumented and would cause the judge to silently ignore agents that correctly handled this sub-ask). The fix is small and secondary to the headline PR review + clipboard work, so weight is 0.05 (matched to the changelog goal). Escape hatch for the 'no fix needed at base commit' case is included because the user-sim's turn_7 is a verbatim quote of a session where the human had already made the fix locally, so the in-task base state may or may not actually require the fix â€” the goal scores either a real fix OR a documented confirmation that no fix was needed.

decomposition_notes: 1 anti-effort gate (goal_0, weight 0) + 1 core (goal_3, weight 0.35) + 7 secondaries (goal_1, goal_2, goal_4, goal_5, goal_6, goal_7, goal_8 â€” weights 0.15, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.10, 0.05, 0.05). Behavioral weights sum to 1.00. goal_0 enforcement is sandbox-level; under audit a violation invalidates the trial. The shape is intentionally different from v2 (which had 1 core + 3 secondaries) for three reasons: (a) v2 omitted the headline 'produce a structured PR review' deliverable that anchors turn_0 â†’ restored as goal_1; (b) v2 collapsed the dependency-question answer into the code-change goal, making the trial uncheckable against turn_1 â†’ split out as goal_2; (c) v2's relaxed 'any mechanism' phrasing for goal_1 silently absorbed a constraint the user explicitly imposed across turn_1 and turn_6 (no new dep) â†’ split out as goal_5. Weights here are rubric-internal completeness weights, NOT the F2P weights in tests/test_manifest.yaml; the manifest's F2P split (0.16 photon-removed / 0.16 native-PNG-primitives / 0.48 behavioral / 0.20 upstream-vitest) is a separate scoring artifact at a different abstraction layer and is intentionally allowed to diverge. All oracle-specific identifiers (clipboard-image.ts, interactive-mode.ts, convertToPng, isSupportedImageMimeType, photon module path, contributor handle, PR number, exact CHANGELOG sentence, clipboard-image-bmp-conversion.test.ts) are kept out of goal text; where the user named photon in turn_1, the goal text refers to it because it is a USER token, not an oracle token. README.md was empty so goal sourcing is turn_0 instruction.md + turns_1-8 user messages, cross-checked against the repo's package manifests where verifiability requires it.
_self_critique_notes: Coverage check: every user message turn 0-8 is anchored by at least one goal â€” turn_0â†’goal_1+goal_7, turn_1â†’goal_2, turn_2â†’goal_1 (merge recommendation), turn_3â†’goal_4, turn_4â†’goal_4, turn_5â†’goal_3+goal_4, turn_6â†’goal_5, turn_7â†’goal_8 (second clause: npm run dev fix; first clause 'tested it works, commit and push' is approval, satisfied by goals 3+4+5 being met), turn_8â†’goal_7. PASS.

No-oracle-leak check: goal text contains no reference to clipboard-image.ts (uses 'clipboard-reading module'), no convertToPng/isSupportedImageMimeType, no @lightningRalf or @earendil-works, no PR number, no specific encoder library name in normative position. 'photon' appears in goal_2 because it is a USER token from turn_1, not an oracle implementation choice. PASS.

Anti-gaming check: (a) goal_3 says 'callers should not have to branch on mime type' â€” prevents the 'add a wrapper in interactive-mode.ts that checks mime' regression; (b) goal_5 says 'New npm dep violates' â€” prevents quietly adding photon if it's not already declared at base; (c) goal_6 says 'must not be skipped, not gated behind CI-only flag' â€” prevents skip-marked stub tests; (d) goal_6 says 'bytes whose signature decodes as that format' â€” prevents hard-coded magic-byte assertion that the conversion code itself fakes; (e) goal_7 says 'NOT inside an already-released version section' â€” prevents the trivially-passing edit to a frozen version block. PASS.
Weights â‰  F2P weights check: rubric is 0.35/0.15/0.10/0.10/0.10/0.10/0.05/0.05; manifest F2P is 0.16/0.16/0.48/0.20 with 3 weight-0 P2Ps. Distinct distributions. PASS.

goal_0 placement check: appears as id goal_0, tier anti_effort, weight 0.0, with explicit 'gating only' framing and a non-rubric (sandbox) enforcement mechanism named. Sum-to-1 invariant on behavioral goals (1-8) verified: 0.35 + 0.15 + 0.10 + 0.10 + 0.10 + 0.10 + 0.05 + 0.05 = 1.00. PASS.

Phase-B re-read: v2 had goal_1 phrased 'into a supported format (PNG)' which subtly biases towards PNG as the only acceptable target; v3 goal_3 generalizes to 'a supported format' since the codebase's SUPPORTED_IMAGE_MIME_TYPES list spans png/jpeg/webp/gif and converting BMP to (say) WEBP would also satisfy the user's literal ask. Kept the generalization.

_dropped_from_v2.why_split: v2 conflated the behavioral conversion outcome with the 'any-mechanism-including-existing-library' relaxation, which let a submission satisfying the surface behavior with a new dep score full credit despite directly contradicting turn_1 + turn_6. Split into goal_3 (behavioral outcome, target-format-generic) and goal_5 (no-new-dep constraint).

_dropped_from_v2.why_change: Same behavioral intent, but anti-gaming language added ('must not be skipped, not gated behind CI-only flag', 'bytes whose signature decodes as that format' rather than just 'matches PNG magic bytes', because a submission could trivially hard-code 0x89504E47 into the conversion stub without performing a valid encode).

_added_for_coverage.covers: Anti-effort / exfiltration gate. Inherited from v2 as goal_0 (also weight 0). v3 adds concrete violation signals derived from this specific trial's turn-2 trajectory (220K-line diff, git fetch of contributor fork, FETCH_HEAD checkout) and is explicit that enforcement is sandbox-level.
_added_for_coverage.anchors_to: Implicit invariant of any benchmark task. Made explicit because the task's task.toml has allow_internet=true and the .git/config in the sandbox points at the live upstream.

_added_for_coverage.covers: The written PR review deliverable (Good/Bad/Ugly + Change summary + Tests + merge recommendation + changelog-exists check).
_added_for_coverage.anchors_to: turn_0 instruction.md (entire output template) and turn_2 ('good to merge?'). v2 omitted this entirely.

_added_for_coverage.covers: Correctly answering the 'did this add a new dependency, didn't we move away from photon?' question.
_added_for_coverage.anchors_to: turn_1 verbatim. v2 had no goal targeting this question.

_added_for_coverage.covers: The 'implement locally, no new dependency' constraint.
_added_for_coverage.anchors_to: turn_6 verbatim, reinforced by turn_1. v2 explicitly relaxed this in its rationale ('the oracle uses the existing photon library for conversion, but the user said implement locally (T7) so native implementations are also valid â€” the rubric is implementation-agnostic'); v3 takes the user at their word and makes the constraint binding.

_self_critique_log.audit_finding: Two verifier-correctness risks flagged by Sonnet's final-review pass: (1) auto_gate_bridge boost in tests/test.sh fires the 0.80 reward whenever len(relevant) >= 2 with relevant = any path starting with 'packages/'. An exfiltration trial (goal_0 violation â€” the documented Opus 4.6 pi-mono-auto-90748a5d__7pFELnX case fetched the contributor fork wholesale, producing a 220K-line diff over 902 files spanning the monorepo) trivially satisfies this threshold and receives an undeserved 0.80 base reward. (2) FAIL_TO_PASS in install_config.json is pinned to the literal vitest description 'converts BMP to PNG on Wayland/WSLg', which contradicts goal_6's mechanism-agnostic language ('Test file name, framework, and assertion style are unconstrained'). A legitimate native-implementation trial with a differently-named test scores 0.0 on reward.txt despite satisfying the goal â€” misleading the Step-0 pilot signal. Sonnet's third headline concern (SANDBOX_ENFORCEMENT_GAP / Dockerfile-level outbound block) is explicitly out of scope per the patch instruction: user is handling it separately and Dockerfile must not be touched.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Two surgical verifier patches in harbor_tasks/pi-mono-auto-90748a5d/tests/. (1) test.sh auto_gate_bridge: scoped the 'relevant' file set from packages/ (entire monorepo) to packages/coding-agent/ (the in-task package only); added an exfiltration guard that refuses the boost when len(all_changed) > 20 OR any file is outside packages/coding-agent/ + repo-root CHANGELOG.md. The refusal is logged for audit and preserves the legacy reward â€” the boost is not negated, just gated behind a diff-shape sanity check. The 20-file threshold is well above the canonical task's 2-3-file footprint (clipboard module + test + CHANGELOG) and well below the exfiltration trial's 902 files, so legitimate ~5-10-file refactors still boost while wholesale exfiltrations do not. (2) install_config.json + test.sh: added FAIL_TO_PASS_REGEX list with four case-insensitive patterns covering 'bmp.*png', 'clipboard.*bmp', 'clipboard.*png', 'wslg?.*(bmp|png)'; updated test.sh's matching logic to treat the F2P as one gate that passes if EITHER the exact-name test OR any test whose name matches a regex passed. Exact-name remains the primary path for canonical-style trials; regex tier rescues mechanism-agnostic alternatives. No Dockerfile changes (user is handling sandbox-enforcement gap separately).

_self_critique_log.audit_finding: The rubric silently drops several explicit asks from the Turn 0 instruction, including the docs check (Step 5), linked issues check (Step 2), and the 'Questions or Assumptions' section of the required output format (Step 7). These omissions are neither scored nor documented in the exclusions. Single biggest risk: agents that completely ignore these will receive full credit for the PR review deliverable.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Expanded goal_1 from 4 sub-clauses (a-d: Good/Bad/Ugly, changelog-exists, merge-recommendation, Change summary + Tests) to 7 sub-clauses, adding (e) linked-issues check anchored to instruction Step 2, (f) docs check anchored to instruction Step 5, and (g) Questions/Assumptions section anchored to instruction Step 7. Sub-clauses are independently scorable so partial credit reflects partial coverage. Goal_1 weight unchanged at 0.15 â€” the additional sub-clauses tighten judging within an already-existing goal rather than carving out new weight; rebalancing would have required reducing goal_3 below 0.35 which crosses the 'headline behavioral ask' threshold. Updated goal_1 rationale to enumerate the eight instruction Steps and cite the v3-rev1 expansion.

_self_critique_log.audit_finding: The rubric silently drops the second half of turn_7 ('also made a fix so npm run dev works for coding-agent, can commit that as well'). There is no goal for this fix or its commit, and the omission is not documented.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: Added goal_8 (secondary, weight 0.05) covering the npm run dev fix for the coding-agent package, with an explicit escape hatch for the case where no fix is needed at the task base commit. Weight rebalanced by reducing goal_3 from 0.40 to 0.35. Behavioral weights still sum to 1.00. Updated _self_critique_notes coverage check to anchor turn_7's second clause to goal_8.

_self_critique_log.audit_finding: The user explicitly named `interactive-mode.ts` (turn_4) and `readClipboardImage` (turn_5), but the rubric abstracts them to 'REPL/TUI/interactive layer' and 'clipboard-read entry point', under-pinning the user's explicit constraints.
_self_critique_log.fix_applied: goal_3: pinned the user-named symbol `readClipboardImage` (turn_5) as the in-scope entry point when that exact symbol or its direct successor exists in the codebase. goal_4: pinned the user-named file `interactive-mode.ts` (turn_4) as a specific forbidden location for the conversion logic. Generic-abstraction wording is retained as a fallback so the goal still scores trials where the codebase has been refactored, but the user-named tokens are now load-bearing in the goal text.

_task_quality_flags.source: harbor_default_rubric.toml (programmatic subset) + per-trial audit of pi-mono-auto-90748a5d__7pFELnX

flags.internet_with_live_origin: Dockerfile has live github.com clone AND task.toml sets allow_internet=true. Allows git-ops exfiltration of merged upstream fix. Confirmed exercised by this trial's turn-2 (git fetch of contributor fork â†’ FETCH_HEAD checkout â†’ 220,787-line incremental patch). Mitigation MUST be sandbox-level (frozen origin mirror, outbound block to upstream + known contributor forks); rubric-level mitigation (goal_0) only documents the gate, it does not enforce it.
flags.multi_deliverable_task: Task arc has three distinct deliverables (written review, code change with architectural+dependency constraints, changelog entry). v2's 4-goal decomposition compressed this into the code change only; v3 expands to 7 behavioral goals + 1 anti-effort gate so each deliverable is independently scorable.
